title: Project Report: SOC Alert Triage Playbook — Cybersecurity Analyst
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Build SIEM detection rules, triage 10 alert scenarios, write IR playbook, conduct tabletop exercise.
objectives: 5 SIEM detection rules (Sigma or Splunk SPL)
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**SOC Alert Triage Playbook** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for Cybersecurity Analyst. Duration: 4–5 weeks. Build SIEM detection rules, triage 10 alert scenarios, write IR playbook, conduct tabletop exercise. Tools: Sigma, Splunk/Elastic, NIST IR.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: 5 SIEM detection rules (Sigma or Splunk SPL)
Objective: Triage playbook for 10 alert types
Objective: Incident response playbook
Objective: Tabletop exercise report
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Cybersecurity Analyst portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Cybersecurity Analyst resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Cybersecurity Analyst resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Detect
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Detect** *(Level: Advanced)*

Write detection rules. Complete these tasks: Brute force; Data exfil; Malware beacon. Deliverable for this phase: Rule definitions.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Brute force
- [ ] Data exfil
- [ ] Malware beacon
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Detect is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Cybersecurity Analyst agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Rule definitions to the teacher.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Detection rules; Triage playbook; IR playbook; Tabletop report. Skills demonstrated: SIEM, Detection, Incident response. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Detection rules
Deliverable: Triage playbook
Deliverable: IR playbook
Deliverable: Tabletop report
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Detection rules and passes the rubric.
